examples.title: gigant/pdfvqa validation sample 0
examples.question: What is the left section about?
examples.predicted_answer: The left section of the image is titled "Background" and discusses secondary abdominal compartment syndrome (ACS) in trauma patients. It mentions that ACS is a well-known complication in critically ill patients characterized by a potentially life-threatening elevation of intra-abdominal pressure (IAP) and subsequent deterioration of organ function. The text also references the World Society of the Abdominal Compartment Syndrome's definition of ACS as sustained IAP greater than 20 mmHg, which can be measured intraperitoneally via a urinary catheter. Additionally, it notes that ACS is differentiated from primary ACS, which is defined by an intra-abdominal bleeding process directly increasing the IAP.
examples.rationale: The reference answer says the left section is simply “Abstract,” but the candidate invents detailed content about abdominal compartment syndrome that is not supported by the reference.
